column4: Unit makes a cracking noise from the tines when it is heating up in dry mode
column12: The PTC ceramic block expands after being heated to a high temperature of ~250C and releases internal stress by cracking causing the "cracking sound"
column13: Add a process at the production line to heat the unit at dry mode 20s to eliminate the PTC cracking sound.
column14: Jul/13/2023 9:05 AM;61d894d8e7637900682a7e8f;[~accountid:61131c64a539cb0068f7c5c1] [~accountid:5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc]  Please help review for RCCA as soon as possible
column15:
Jul/13/2023 1:35 PM;61d894d8e7637900682a7e8f;[~accountid:61131c64a539cb0068f7c5c1] [~accountid:5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc] This issue seems to only be occurring on first use of Dry Mode. I have tested a few units where I heard the cracking sound the first time they were turned on and the cracking sound did not occur again even after the unit was fully cool. Can you please help to run some tests on your end of:

-turning on Dry Mode first use to see if the cracking sound is present

-If the cracking sound is present, let the unit cool completely then turn it into Dry Mode again to see if cracking sound occurs. Repeat this process to ensure cracking sound is only present at first use.

If we determine cracking sound is only present at first use, can we add a step into the process line to let each unit heat up completely in Dry Mode then cool before shipping them out? That way we could prevent the cracking sound from happening in people’s homes.
column16: Jul/24/2023 6:03 AM;61131c64a539cb0068f7c5c1;[~accountid:62ea1bdb1323922c61e1dec4] pls help update RCCA.
column17: Aug/30/2023 1:55 AM;62ea1bdb1323922c61e1dec4;PTC ceramic block cracked during first-time use resulting in “Cracking Sound”.
column18:
Nov/24/2023 1:28 AM;62ea1bdb1323922c61e1dec4;[~accountid:615c5172d9820f0070af4d12] 

Solution 1: Change the PTC ceramic block to 5 pieces from 3 pieces, cost up +$0.23/unit; 

Solution 2: Heating equipment pre-heat, equipment costs $12,800

​The solution does not align from the Boston side, and as this sound occurs only in the first use and has no performance and safety impact, suggest closing this ticket.

[~accountid:61d894d8e7637900682a7e8f]  FYI.

column4: HD400 Leshow introduce cost down PCBA and motor(ECN20230418-210006)-Bare motor life test_1/16 unit(186#) tested, found the motor Stop working @440h.
column12:
After change new PCBA, the motor can work.  

Parts content of FW was erased illegally,  it was led by two factors, the one is flash operating times no enough margin, the other is the Vcc is not stable when the operating for erase/write flash.
column13:
Cyclic addresses are written for flash data, so the flash cycle of operating times will be raised from 1,000cycle to 32,000cycle,

Add extra 100ms delay before initialization to ensure the voltage is stable when operating flash.

column12: PTC back cover broke during ABR 25th drop test . The broken material fell into the motor blade housing, causing the motor to damage.
column13:
The root cause is PTC back cover was broken during 25th drop test. 

Due to the material chosen limit of high temperature resistance(PPS 160C), It is easy to broken during drop test. but it is low risk after 25th drop test. Suggest updating the test standard and no action needed for this.

column4: MB2_1/3(497#), found LED4 flashing after power on unit.
column12: The BLDC PCBA was broken due to the  R172 on the BLDC PCBA was missing by collision.
column13: Be careful not to damage the PCBA when disassembling and installing the unit.
column14: May/10/2023 8:31 AM;61a81ea2b43d5b006a6fc742;[~accountid:60b6ebaa66df790070562c37] LED4 flashing indicates motor is blocked.  After replacing with a new motor the issue still existed. The  broken BLDC PCBA has been sent back to GB for further analysis to locate the defects and find the root cause.

!DOLCE-403-MB2.mp4|width=720,height=1280!
column12: LED2,3,5,6 and IQ light on after 13 ABR cycle, interference issue between ionizer and EE PCBA
column13:
1.block the hole on the mica plate of the heater due to the discharge needle and the fuse right next to each other through the hole.

2.optimizing the SW for interference issue with SW V08.01 for MP
column14: May/10/2023 9:32 PM;61a81ea2b43d5b006a6fc742;IQ&LED2&LED3&LED5&LED6 Flash indicates MCU  HardFault error,  The defect unit could recover by power off  and power on . It could be related Ionizer . EE is working with GB to find the root cause.
column15: May/11/2023 8:11 AM;60ca14c620d2dd0070c5e736;[~accountid:61a81ea2b43d5b006a6fc742] Hi, is it possible to please provide a date when we can see an update on the root cause?
column16: May/12/2023 8:58 AM;61a81ea2b43d5b006a6fc742;@[~accountid:60ca14c620d2dd0070c5e736] Hi , the root cause is that the Ionizer spray the anion to the fuse in the heater through the discharge needle.
column17: May/23/2023 7:51 AM;6124bfff0952180069d65b9d;[~accountid:61a81ea2b43d5b006a6fc742] thanks for this update. Now that the team understands the root cause, what changes need to be made to mitigate this issue? Do we need to relocate the discharge needle?
column18: May/24/2023 8:50 AM;61a81ea2b43d5b006a6fc742;[~accountid:6124bfff0952180069d65b9d] 1. The temporary measure is to block the hole on the mica plate of the heater due to the discharge needle and the fuse right next to each other through the hole.  2.. To relocate the discharge needle for long run. 3.From EE side, optimizing the SW for  interference elimination  is on going.

Apr/18/2023 5:12 AM;60891091ccda29006b77ff85;[~accountid:5fc5b70f0d2f61006f98e31c] All bare motor life passed, but unit life failed. The failure is by motor winding burnt and short. Root cause is motor temperature in unit is higher than bare motor. The improvement is to reduce motor temperature. 

The improvement action, 

# Lamination level upgrade, thickness changed from 0.2mm to 0.15mm
# Process control, control applying adhesive position, not cover motor lamination slot, winding heat is easy to be flow out. 

The improved samples can be ready by 4/19, please verify test in unit ASAP. Thanks!

column17:
Apr/22/2023 11:35 PM;5fc6eb47facfd6007634e0cf;[~accountid:5be0519a741c7e2e7e9dae7b] After we received the unit,checked this unit and found the resistance value between U and VS is 30.1R, V and VS is 3.13K, W and VS is 131.6K,  normal the resistance between U/V/W and VS is about 212.6k;  so the MOS has broken. after replaced the MOS, the unit works well.

After analysis, we have optimized the three parts of the software:

# After power off, the motor will slide for 500ms, and then the motor will be brake by MCU, which will generate a high current. The new software algorithm will reduce the brake current, so that avoid the high current to damage the motor. 
# when normal running, if the current difference between U/V/W wires exceeds 2 times, it means the motor works an abnormality. the unit will be protection and stop work;  
# when power on, we found sometime the unit will not start, after tested and  measured the curve of motor current, there is also a small probability of high current and startup failure. 

the 1 and 2 has updated in to FW V7.02.01 and be used for MB2;

the 3 has updated into FW V7.03.01. 

Please see attached Picture named FW version update.

Aug/07/2023 10:29 PM;61bbe629f19b53006aa2b0c8;[~accountid:61d894d8e7637900682a7e8f]  [~accountid:61131c64a539cb0068f7c5c1] [~accountid:5d790ca547387e0d2bcd4d5f]  

It caused by handle parts different water content. We will arrange the updated sample to team review.

There is a process for add water content after PA66 GF30 handle molding, to meet SN material strength testing requirements.  If the water steam timing not enough or exceed, it will be can’t meet the material strength requirement, and the color will be minor changed. In order to control the variable, let color stabilize on MP, we have taken below actions:

* Firstly, we requested supplier that it need fixed the parameter and machine when molding, keep the color consistency
* Then, keep the balance between color and ball drop testing request, fixed the water steam timing for parts, and keep water cleaning
* At the end, let supplier compare the handle color with the approved sample, monitor the color consistency when production in time

column4: LED light color when heating up looks green instead of yellow. Consumer feedback has shown that most consumers think the yellow light looks green
column12:
The yellow light here comes from the combination of red and green colors. When the green light is too strong, the combination color will be biased towards green.

Gradient issue is caused by red and green mixture
column13:
Reduce the intensity of the green light source.

Add additional semi translucent silicone part or modify Lens to extend light pipe to close to LED.

column15:
Mar/07/2023 1:59 AM;60891091ccda29006b77ff85;[^整机寿命分析报告 230304.pdf]

[~accountid:61131c64a539cb0068f7c5c1] Attachment is G-motor analysis report. FYI.

EB0 motor life tested with unit PCBA circuit, the motor 30V DC voltage is supplied by AC circuit rectified, rectified voltage wave is worse than constant DC voltage. This leads carbon brush wore fast. 

Another, EB0 life test condition is severe. Motor is continues running, motor has no rest for cooling down. This is another short life reason.

The bare motor life test with 30Vdc constant power supplier and cyclyes of 20' on+10' off were performed in G-motor lab before, life time is about 300hours.

We have discussed life test cycle recent, some evaluation is starting in Leshow and G-motor lab, hope it can be help to define a more reasonable test cycles.

My another concern, unit life spec is 300hours, it is a challenge for this type PMDC motor. Motor factory is still exploring new carbon material for 300hours target. Please further check whether it can be revised based on EB0/EB1 bare motors and units more life data. Thanks!

column13:

column22:
Jun/27/2023 10:45 PM;5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc;[~accountid:61d894d8e7637900682a7e8f] Attach a section for description, as the snap with flex arm (#1) to ensure the hook can be inserted and no break during assembling, but the room is limit in red circle and lead to the arm is short and overlap to be small (#2) to make sure no break for insertion and to keep high efficiency for daily production line output, that’s why the snap will pop out during drop impact. actually  we made prototype in previously to increase overlap by 0.2-0.3, but it’s very hard to insert and snap broken, so we suggest to get team align to close this ticket based on improvement on EB2.2.

!20221108-012037-05bc40fa.mp4|width=368,height=640!
column12:
This phenomenon has nothing to do with FW V7.12 that fixing LED4 problems.  

the unit is placed in 40C/90% humidity environment box more than 16H, then the surface of Heat NTC and PCBA is covered with water mist, the NTC value is equivalent to short circuit to GND, After power on, MCU detects NTC short-circuited value(NTC temp>200C) and last 5s, unit stop works and show LED456 flash. 

the NTC temp is 130C when normal work at HH&HF mode;  short-circuited protection function be triggered when the NTC temp>200C * 5s; 

we can’t change the values 200C*5s due to safe.

column12:
The material PPS A504X90 with High RTI (>200C) and low impact resistance, tine cover hardly to withstand x30 drops.
Based on failed sample review, the crack happened on welding line.
column13:
EB action-(P.S implemented before MB)

# Injection parameter adjust to increase tooling temperature if possible.
# Injection gate change to x5 points from 3 points to change welding line location and move down welding line from teeth to bottom.
# Add ribs and material on the two side teeth and increase the radius to improve the strength.

Improvement after MP

# Add x4 trapezoidal bosses on brush base and increase PTC heater back cover to support Tine Assy.
# Add material to  tine cover sunken area to increase strength
# Add material 0.8mm inside of the tooth near the handle side
# Extend the length of the rib

column15:
Oct/25/2022 6:53 AM;624f8ec9f6a2690069620ea4;In common operation condition, if the rotation stopper got broken then the lock must be also simultaneous broken, real case is only rotation stopper got broken, so it cannot rule out the possibility of operator’s misoperation, i.e., tester haven’t released the button to resetting position before every 180degree rotation ending, moreover , turn to opposite direction is easier to broken the stopper  

on the other hand, for rotation stopper, it’s too limited space to add plastic on or other redesign, so even if we enlarge R angle a little but eventually it cannot bear all force on the stopper, so back to square one, test’s misoperation is root cause, at present no other simple good solution

Long term

Arrange the prototype on the hard tool to validate the drop testing, ECD 6/22

the rotation of concentrator is tighten. both HD400US and HD400INTL have the same issue.

refer to attached.
column12:
1. O-ring loose fit with slider holder 
2. The overlap for snap on bottom cover is too big and connector material is PA66-GF30 which is too strong and easy to make the snap deformation
column13:
1. Decrease the inner diameter for O ring to 26.5mm from 29.3mm
2. change the material for connector to PA66-GF15 from PA66-GF30 and soak the connector into water to make it softer when assembly
3. Decrease 0.3mm overlap for snap

column28:
Jul/08/2022 11:44 AM;61a81ea2b43d5b006a6fc742;@[~accountid:6166916772f6970069d12923] I have checked this issue  unit whose S/N is  375# instead of 275# at the lab today . I asked the same tester to show me how she does the  inlet block test and to reproduce the issue.  Please note that the tester started the time only when she had fully  blocked the inlet.  This was not correct. The tester should start the time immediately when she blocked the inlet roughly more than half. I attached a video for your reference.  From the video, the tester found the inlet protection triggered after 5S after inlet being blocked.  In fact  it should be 9S if you double check it. 9S is ok because it is more than 8S which is set by the FW.  If you find the inlet block triggered less than 8S  after inlet being blocked it will be the issue.[~accountid:5d7913018b0e290c45aa2dd9]  FYI.

* Can the cap fall off on the unit that was used in the drop test?

8/18, Yes, the end cap can be pushed back to current position, the gap will return and close to previous value.

column12:
1. 0.25 gap b/w bottom cover and body can pull bottom cover and cause deformation.
2. Screw holder area is not strong enough, it can deform when fix screw.
3. The back cover can wobble due to no limit.
column13:
1.  Remedy the 0.25mm gap b/w screw boss and body.
2. Add material to screw holder area to make it robust.
3. Add ribs on top cap to stop back cover wobbling.

column4:
1.The buttons fixed by glue which are not reworkable --high scrap cost in process (according to LS previous experience estimated 3~4% defect rate for each buttons, total 6~8% defect rate)

2.Glue value is not controllable due to quick cure glue, which will case uneven surface for the buttons

3. There is no fixed structure, it will be misalignment for the buttons when fixed the button by pressing buttons



!Glue to fix buttons.jpg|width=552,height=439!
column12: glue process and feasibility improve
column13:
from structure side 

1.Increased the poke-yoke feature for better assemble

2.As there is only 0.06 height difference from front to back on button, so the poke-yoke feature changed to allow button can be assembled even turning 180° to improve manufacture, (only poke-yoke for left and right side)

3. Add 4x ribs on the rubble to increase contact area to improve the button wobble issue

For the glue, LX recommended glue is used on many similar product, also LX is searching some backup glue and validate on dome switch prototype with EB1

Further action for Hybrid building EB1.5:
1.  Button top surface change to flat no need poke-yoka for locating,
2. Button bottom suface change to flat no poke-yoka for sticking.

column15:
Mar/09/2022 1:44 AM;5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc;[^RE_ Omni-Styler StandUp - 08_03_2022 (9c3b85be-c3f3-4403-b9d5-e9bd2f65e805).msg]

Attached CN comments, the relationship for air flow, outlet temp and power will be Air Flow {color:#ff5630}*↑*{color} , outlet temp @1’’ will {color:#ff5630}*↓*{color}, so heater power {color:#ff5630}*↑*{color} to ensure outlet temp within spec, but NTT and TCO/Fuse spec need to re-validate firstly to ensure there is no safety issue.

[~accountid:5cbe1c2dcf9b780e6a39fd1e] [~accountid:5b523c3a628cd32ca1bee0ea] Could team confirm which parameter is primary requirement, air flow or outlet temp? if current configuration(365outlet grill + ionizer removed) cannot pass NTT test, (initial feedback from EE the power is 1440W for current config with 100.8C outlet temp), so the next mitigation plan from CN recommendation will be to reduce power to meet below two scenarios:

Scenario A: Lower power with lower outlet temp but keep the highest air flow.

Scenario B:: Lower power with lower air flow but keep the same outlet temp.

May/23/2022 1:29 AM;60ffac188ad5b60070804cd6;@[~accountid:5ece92c0c761b10c33e0989d]  Checked that the width of waist-type hole in AL sleeve for EB1 is 0.14mm narrower than EB0 and as a result, the outlet area of EB1 round brush is 230mm^2 smaller than EB0.

 The reason for this is that EB0 AL sleeve is made by laser cutting and EB1 is made by stamping tooling.

There is a dimensional deviation between the two processes. 

In addition, EB1 waist-type hole dimensions are within the tolerance range(2.5±0.1mm).  Could you please adjust the KPI to 240 passes?

column4: In P2 the heater is rotated 60° off where it should sit. The heater was designed to have a mostly solid central wall and the other mica sheets then have large holes in to help diffuse the airflow upwards and downwards.
column12: More air enters the heater on the top than the bottom. Heater layout impacts how the heater will diffuse the temperature
column13: Rotate the heater by 60° to align the heater central mica with the centre plane of the Main Body.
column14:
Dec/14/2021 8:40 AM;6021766ebe86a0006904a2f0;[~accountid:5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc]  Currently the central wall is offset by 60° 

!image-20211214-133843.png|width=1036,height=788!

The heater needs to be rotated by 60° to bring the central heater mica back inline with the central plane of the product:

!image-20211214-133920.png|width=1185,height=789!

This will rotate the NTC by 60° at the same time which means the straight vs. dryer format testing will have to be re-run as the outlet temperatures could be affected with the NTC in the correct position. [~accountid:60ae5993f601b0006a765023] [~accountid:606b1cf13a5163007647544c] [~accountid:5e78a0ca2864400c2ac5bade]  FYI
column15: Dec/14/2021 8:53 AM;606b1cf13a5163007647544c;[~accountid:5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc]  [~accountid:5be0519b8995b717bb1ba0c4]  can this be fixed for P2? This heater position is likely contributing to flow/temperature unevenness. One of the main primaira recommendations was to keep the mica wall with the fuses vertical in the device. If we need to move the NTC, recommend to keep the heater position the same but move the NTC on the heater assembly rather than rotating the entire heater. FYI [~accountid:6151dbaa9cdb9300720dedc3] [~accountid:5d791304175d7b0c359d2d1e]

Jan/07/2022 7:50 AM;6021766ebe86a0006904a2f0;Hi [~accountid:5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc] , sorry for not being clear. In the heater there are three mica sheets. One of these sheets has smaller holes and holds the TCO and fuses:

!image-20220107-124140.png|width=1129,height=898!

!image-20220107-124201.png|width=1073,height=934!

It’s this mica sheet (in green above) that we would like to sit vertically in the airpath.

+*Issue/Risk:*+ Currently, the wrong mica sheet is sitting vertically - it’s one with the larger punched holes - *This is incorrect and will affect how the heat is distributed around the heater in different dryer formats*:

!image-20220107-124336.png|width=50%!

+*Proposal:*+ We would like to rotate the heater by 60° and have the correct mica sheet sitting vertically in the airflow. This will help more evenly distribute the heat in both dryer formats, “straight” and “7”:

!image-20220107-124526.png|width=819,height=592!

This also has the benefit of rotating the NTC to a more central position:

!image-20220107-124719.png|width=670,height=864!

_Note: The heater should still line up with the ribs on the outlet as we have rotated a full 60°_

Please let me know if the ask is clear, Thanks.

column27:
Apr/11/2022 6:58 AM;6021766ebe86a0006904a2f0;[~accountid:5a28ae3cba0e0b386ccb93dc]  The concern is that there are two “zones” in the heater that are separated by the fuse/TCO wall but the heater has air directed from the flow diverter upstream that sits on a horizontal plane. The current scheme could see uneven heating between zones in the “7” or “Straight” mode if the air is biased into zone 1 or 2. (The assumption was that we will never achieve a true 50:50 flow split with the flow diverter.)

The preferred scheme means that there +*if*+ there is biased airflow from the flow diverter in “straight” and/or “7” mode, that each side of the heater is fed the same volume of airflow and so the output from the heater is constant for both zones. 


!image-20220411-104619.png|width=1362,height=561!


By rotating the heater by 60 degrees we are allowing the system to have some tolerance as I suspect the air is not divided equally between the upper and lower areas of the diverter:

!image-20220411-104907.png|width=1244,height=612!

I agree that re-validation will need to occur but the original heater was designed with the “preferred” suggestion above to be primary path.

[~accountid:606b1cf13a5163007647544c] FYI

However, from the thermal plot uploaded on 04/12 it looks like there is a huge variance in the temperatures around the heater. The NTC looks to be positioned in the hottest area. but the coolest area (-30°C) is 180° away on the opposite side of the heater. Is it possible/quick to test the heater with the NTC in say 6 different orientations? As the NTC is sat in the hottest air, this may be limiting the heater as it thinks it has hit the desired temperature (100°C). If you move the NTC into a cooler zone it may keep the heater running until that “Cold” zone reaches 100°C. This may push the hottest zone to be more than 100 degrees but on average it could help the outlet temps

Unit 2 with S/N: U12Q3109Z5A0 - This unit would not work at all completely inoperable, reset switch could be depressed but no effect. Tore down plug, had a burning smell. Excess solder was found in position 'SCR2'. [CAR |https://quality.euro-pro.com/Lists/New%20CAR/Item/editifs.aspx?List=7c4f58c5%2Dca32%2D403f%2D8310%2De9ad9680397e&ID=6112&Source=https%3A%2F%2Fquality%2Eeuro%2Dpro%2Ecom%2FLists%2FNew%2520CAR%2FAll%2520Items%2Easpx%23InplviewHashdd761b4d%2D2a75%2D446f%2D88c2%2Dea94b274de9e%3DWebPartID%253D%257BDD761B4D%2D%2D2A75%2D%2D446F%2D%2D88C2%2D%2DEA94B274DE9E%257D&Web=f102e3e6%2D48da%2D4fdb%2D81c4%2Dfa9502582c32]initiated to establish process improvements.
column12:
Why this issue happened?
Y1: Why did the LED flashing continuously, and the auto-monitoring function prompts the ALCI fault? 
A: The ALCI circuit is faulty
Y2: Why does the ALCI circuit fail? 
A: Open circuit the secondary coil of ZCT(1.99KΩ between two pin of ZCT). 
Y3: Why is the ZCT secondary coil open? 
A: One of the ZCT pin root connection coil is not reliable. 
Y4: Why is the ZCT pin root connection coil unreliable? 
A: ZCT pin root coil is not welded. 
Y5: Why is the ZCT pin root coil not welded? 
A: To avoid scald the ZCT housing. 
2. Why the defect not detected?
2. After the root coil of ZCT pin is open circuit, the copper wire is still on the pin, and ZCT can be turned on normally. power on and stand by for 5 seconds, and the test does not detect it.

3. Why the system allowed?
3. Design issue, didn’t consider there is no solder on root of the mutual inductor pin will caused secondary coil open.
column13:
CA.PA
1.1 Change coil line warp position from middle position to end of root which easy soldering.-Done WK4121
1.2 Leading in auto equipment instead manual operate.-Done WK5021
2. Update SOP of power on and stand by for 5 seconds. Knock twice on the rubber pad before each process testing, for low voltage test, press repeatedly for 10 times.-Done 10/6
3. Lesson learnt to new SKUs-Done 12/11

column4:
Kapton tape and thermal grease are being used on the motor power FETs (CS14N25A4R) . Kapton tape will not dissipate the heat generated by the FETs because it is an thermal insulator.


!image-20211005-171403.png|width=353,height=419!
column14: Nov/19/2021 10:23 PM;5cbe1c2dcf9b780e6a39fd1e;This ticket was opened because we have components we possibly don’t need.  Kapton tape will not dissipate heat. If we do need to dissipate heat on these components we need an alternate solution. If not, we need to remove them ideally for a cost savings
column15: Nov/22/2021 4:11 AM;5bd81045bd2c4c2de48b76b3;[~accountid:5cbe1c2dcf9b780e6a39fd1e] Agree with you that the Kapton tape will affect heat dissipation. But the tap is helpful to pass ESD test and can’t be removed. Suzhou lab had NTT test for the MOSFETs, not failure found. [~accountid:5b523c3a628cd32ca1bee0ea]
column16: Nov/22/2021 1:11 PM;5cbe1c2dcf9b780e6a39fd1e; [~accountid:5bd81045bd2c4c2de48b76b3] , have we completed ESD both with and without the tape? If yes, please share the test results. I don’t fully understand how the kapton tape would help dissipate static electricity on the FETs if there is thermal grease in between, unless this thermal grease is also static dissipative.
column17: Nov/23/2021 1:51 PM;603ebe256a01a30069f7304d;[~accountid:5d546aa43f5e050c17177d02] Do we have test results without the Kapton tape for ESD.

column4:
Obsverations when accessory is removed within 2s after unit power off 

Please check if the unit behaved as expected in following case:

Accessory is attached to the unit and default settings have been changed, then power off the unit,
Case a. If remove accessory within ~2s after power off, then attach back the accessory before unit power on, notice that unit will go to Mode Default Settings after power on. 

Case b. If remove accessory greater than ~2s after power off, then attach back the accessory before unit power on, notice that unit will remain in Last Used Settings after power on. 

Please refer to the video for details.

Issue was originally reported for GB Alternate Design. Confirmed same issue occurs on KCL MP, KCL SPC1068 EB1 (Alternate Design 1) / GB STMG EB1 (Alternate Design 2)
column12: UI flow definition
column13: FW Updated with case b.
column14:
Aug/30/2021 3:26 PM;5d7913018b0e290c45aa2dd9;[~accountid:5b523c3a628cd32ca1bee0ea]  Could PD provides input on this issue? We are observing inconsistent behavior on the IQ functionality. 

# If the accessory is re-attached while use, regardless of the previous mode of operation, the mode returns to the “accessory default” mode.
# If the accessory is re-attached while power-off, the mode returns to the “remembered setting”.



The unit takes a couple of seconds to fully discharge, circuitry is alive ~2s after turn off. Therefore, what the video demonstrates is the same as scenario 1. 



In summary, should the unit ALWAYS return to the “remembered setting” when the same accessory is removed and attached, instead of the default mode?
column15: Aug/30/2021 6:26 PM;5b523c3a628cd32ca1bee0ea;[~accountid:5d7913018b0e290c45aa2dd9]  - the video that is labeled “greater than 2 seconds” should be the case for all scenarios where the accessory is removed when unit is OFF. In other words, the intent here was that if someone shuts off the unit, removes the accessory, and replaces it before turning it back on the next time, the dryer should remember their saved setting as if they never removed it.  We didn’t want the unit to ALWAYS return if someone keeps the unit running and removes it/replaces it, because then we lose any way to give them IQ - once they change it once (even if by accident), it would forever remember that mode. We want to give an easy way to return to the IQ mode (removing & replacing the accessory while the unit is on). Is it possible to limit this 2s time frame where the circuitry is still live?

column20:
Sep/14/2021 11:43 PM;5b7c1c9f10aacc58d3961949;[~accountid:5cbe1c2dcf9b780e6a39fd1e] [~accountid:5be0519a741c7e2e7e9dae7b]  It is not purely assembly issue. This defect happened several times in production line. The wire is pinched by the sharp metal terminal of grey wire beside the yellow wire. Once touched during power on, it will cause serious consequence-burnt. And the assembly is somehow blind assembly and inside space is quite limited, so it is hard to avoid that the wire could be squeezed together during assembly.

And we must improve it from structure side and we can add glue on the terminal of grey wire to avoid touch during assembly and wire routing.

column4: Unit was used twice by internal employee. Unit made a popping noise and started to emit smoke. On teardown the heating coil is burnt.  Unit still turns on and works but there is a burning smell.
column12:
Foreign object stuck in heating coils. Heater element supplier used multiple material (inside heater, outside heater) to simulate the defect:
Heater material:
- Glass fiber: No fire and burn
- UL1332 wire jacket: burn with loose residue
- Black heat shrink tubing: burn with powder residue 
- Inner rack PA66 material
Package material: 
- Pearl wool: white residue
Outside material: 
- White tape (heater assembly finger cover): burn with white smoke and powder residue
- HD100 unit back cover PC material: Burn with black residue, similar with reported defect
And also found the teardown back cover has one stop block lost, which suspect be later falled into heater element inside. 

- There also other potential material can be burnt like the defect.
column13:
1. The MP OPT heater element design eliminated the middle mica layer, can see the abnormal material during inspection;
2. Heater supplier has implemented visual inspection which included abnormal material since EB period

Aug/04/2023 4:09 AM;605be3516190aa0069d2c38e;[~accountid:61818e0e3e3753006f79682b] We improved the handle cover 3 times and but we still couldn’t pass two test of the power cord *the Cord Wrap Test/Strain relief test* . 

The root cause

The 15AWG PVC cord is harder and smoother than the 16AWG rubber cord, so it is difficult to fasten firmly into the handle cover .  if we further increase material to the ribs in handle cover it is possible to pass test, but the assembly will become very difficult and the cord sheath will also be torn.  HD100 order forecast is less and no value to improve more . we suggest to close this ticket.

column15:
Jul/14/2021 1:40 AM;5b7c1c9f10aacc58d3961949;I carefully checked the case and found there’re 2 related processed with negative ion wire.

First is the incoming material. I checked all the incoming materials in line and found wire skin is quite clear, no any broken mark, so this process is excluded;

Second is assembly process, put the negative ion assy into housing. wire skin will be touched inside wall of housing, but no broken mark as lab found. Next is to assemble motor into housing, motor connector at top side will have chance to touch negative ion wire due to limit space inside housing. Simulated 3pcs and found there’s similar broken mark as lab found. So this is structure issue instead of process issue. See pic below. [~accountid:5bd81045bd2c4c2de48b76b3] 

My suggestion is 1) Accept slight broken mark on line, but I’m not sure the worst case; 2)Reduce negative ion wire length to reduce the touch chance with motor connector.

Pls give your further decision.

column4: on INTL unit, noise changes to high pitched noise when switching to smaller outlet hole on 2in1
column12:
UPDATE: 

The noise can be heard in concentrator mode due to back pressure in the system caused by the restriction of the concentrator nozzle on the 2-in-1. The long term goal is to solve this with changes to the motor and fan. The short term goal is to modify the accessory so the noise is no longer heard.


Archived root cause - Not MP workstream:
The impeller blades of INTL version motor fan has a large inclination angle, air speed fast, easy to crush with air returned from outlet block and result in abnormal noise. UPA
column13:
1. Improve shaft hardness/reduce fitting clearance
2. Reduce gap between stator/housing, bearing/housing/fan balance.
3. change INTL Motor impeller structure same as US version.
4. Change Winding turns to 90T From 185T;
Change winding wire diameter to Φ0.14mm from Φ0.1mm

column12:
1.	The painting environment is too dirty.
2.	The 3rd painting layer (surface layer) dry out parameter is not reasonable, the WIP surface painting is not dry out enough and waiting too long time before going to the 2nd dry out oven. During this period, the surface is easy to adhere the fiber, dust and caused painting defect.
column13:
1.	Supplier to change the painting shop from the old painting shop where the environment is dirty and without automatic machine for painting to new painting shop which has automatic pathway and automatic painting robot; - already implemented during EB2.
2.	Short term action plan: supplier to change the surface painting oven temperature from 50℃ to 70℃, and no waiting time between the 1st and 2nd oven process. - already implemented during EB2.
Long term action plan: supplier to discuss with the painting material vender the recommended dry out temperature and time, and then to try to implement; if not suitable on our product, need to discuss to change the surface painting material to be the same as F6 which they have mature process control parameter.  
3.Search another new supplier who has anti-dust shop.
